raw_text: september 12th, 2016 name matesanz saiz, juan josé gender male date and place of birth 1st november 1991, madrid (spain) nationality spanish mobile phone +34 665 954 756 e-mail juan.matesanz.saizg gmail.com l am an aeronautical engineer passionate, highly motivated and enthusiastic about planes and aircrafts in general. during my professional experience have improved my adaptability to change, problem solving and communication skills. critical thinking, responsibility and teamworking are some characteristics that define myself at the workplace. education 2009-2014 aeronautical engineer at escuela técnica superior de ingenieros aeronáuticos (etsia) - universidad politécnica de madrid (upm) average mark: 7.8 with honours in 4 subjects. master thesis: sloshing and its aeroelastic effects on a wing developed within an industry-university collaboration with airbus military. it was qualified with a 10/10 and selected for the international pegasus- aiaa students conference. professional experience feb 2016 now gnss junior engineer (full-time) gmv aerospace and defence s.a.u. (madrid) gnss operating systems (os) division. work in a multidisciplinary team, responsible for maintenance, development and evolution of the ground segment of the galileo ospf and egnos operating system. in the scope of r&d ive participated in the development of internal analysis tools of the navigation messages. as a result of the maintenance project ive experienced a regular contact with the customer. sept 2015 feb 2016 design engineer intern (full-time) airbus operations s.l. (madrid) flight physics department. aerodynamic design team. gaia internship. my work was focused on the aerodynamic design of fuselage, htp and belly fairing with the purpose of optimizing the plane configuration in order to reduce the drag and fuel consumption. during those months worked on cfd, numerical simulations, postprocessing, development of tools and methods... nov 2014 jun 2015 research engineer intern (full-time) the von karman institute for fluid dynamics (vki) (belgium) 1/2 september 12th, 2016 aeronautics and aerospace department. our team was focused on the thermal exchange during the spacecraft atmospheric re-entry. we worked with continuous feedback from the plasma wind tunnel tests team and r&d departments from important companies of the aerospace industry such as astrium (airbus group) and esa. was responsible for theoretical analysis and numerical simulations (cfd) of the heat transfer during atmospheric re-entry. co-author of a conference paper. february 2015: awarded with an applied research orientation (aro) fellowship. additional experience april 2015 participant of the 11* pegasus-alaa students conference on behalf of the upm, defending my master thesis. ecole de ''air. salon de provence (france) march 2015 conference paper: on the flight extrapolation of stagnation-point ablative material plasma wind tunnel tests for the 8" european symposium for space vehicles (esa) (lisbone) july 2013 wind tunnel engineer intern at idr/upm. (part-time) in charge of several wind tunnel tests in order to select the best wing-tail configuration for innovative drones. skills and abilities language skills: e spanish: mother tongue e english: high working knowledge. at vki and gmv english is the working language. b2 certificate: fce university of cambridge e french: good working knowledge computer skills: lived for several months in the french-speaking region of belgium. french was a working language at vki. b2 certificate: advanced certificate escuela official de idiomas de madrid e advanced level on catia v5. e others: linux, ms office, c, python, bash... additional information e stays in the netherlands, belgium and france. e music band. guitarist. e driving licence (european b level) 2/2
